mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns during rate cuts are not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger equity sell-offs within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE rises by over 0.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in real personal consumption expenditures and housing starts indicate a broad contraction in aggregate demand that depresses corporate earnings expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint declines in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict negative 60-day returns with a statistical significance level of p < 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in the index of industrial production below its 12-month moving average signals a supply-side recession that forces equity markets to price in lower future cash flows, yielding negative forward returns.
failure_condition: The signal loses predictive power during post-shock recovery phases, with 20-day forward returns turning positive in over 60% of triggered cases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly rise in the consumer price index above 0.4 percent triggers immediate inflation fears that compress equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5-day returns remain positive in more than 50% of months where CPIAUCSL increases by over 0.4 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising total nonfarm payrolls and a simultaneously rising unemployment rate indicates labor market inefficiency and hidden slack that suppresses equity valuations over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and UNRATE fails to generate negative 60-day returns with a rank IC exceeding 0.04 in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product directly translates to higher corporate revenue projections, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month as markets price in improved earnings.
failure_condition: Positive GDPC1 growth fails to predict positive 20-day returns, with a walk-forward rank IC below 0.01 over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly decline in industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle signals an abrupt supply shock that forces immediate equity deleveraging and negative S&P 500 returns within the following week.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative 5-day returns during tightening cycles, with positive returns occurring in over 55% of triggered instances.
